contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: [4/6/26, 7:22:44 PM] Gary Teh: Green: is upbeat or humorous 
  
  Blue: is downbeat and somber 
  
  Orange: is educational or just informational. Cerebral
  
  Grey: is just emotional neutral and informational content.
  [4/6/26, 7:36:51 PM] Gary Teh: Been been a bunch of AB testing on my Instagram/facebook account to observe the distribution rate 
  
  Notice content that are somber and emotionally neutral tend not to get as much circulation as content that is deliberately structured to be upbeat or humorous 
  
  The educational quotient of a piece doesn’t really seem to have as much effect on its circulation 
  
  Meanwhile content that are laced to trigger indignation/anger/outrage tend to also get as much circulation. These content are polarizing creating an us versus them dynamic. 
  
  Lately I have been thinking a lot about this final category of content and its relationship to our core values.
  [4/6/26, 11:35:32 PM] Gary Teh:  
  [4/7/26, 5:56:29 AM] Gary Teh: Seems the reach of the nomadic community after the winter season extends very far, as far as the New England region which is on the opposite end of the country from the desert region
  
  https://www.facebook.com/share/1CQ88EkVBi/?mibextid=wwXIfr
  [4/7/26, 6:16:29 AM] Gary Teh: Am actually surprised this post deliberately downbeat and sombre generated inbound interest in the cacao
  [4/7/26, 6:21:10 AM] Gary Teh: Based on the outcome of the experiment on various emotional vibes which I ran in my personal social media account as well as this piece of writing from Andreessen Horowitz it makes sense that future content for Agroverse should lean more upbeat, with slight elements of humor, some education content but not too cerebral/intellectual…
  
  My sense is we should avoid leaning on the use of indignation, contempt and anger as levers for driving content circulation on the social media platforms as these vibes only serve to promote polarization and run counter to our DAO’s core values of mindfulness, compassion and equanimity as well as our purpose of healing the world with love
  [4/7/26, 6:35:50 AM] Gary Teh: Some further food for thought from Gemini
  
  Awe is another one which we can lean into. We are dealing with the Amazon rainforest after all, the vast expansive beauty of nature does has that effect when one is immersed in it
  [4/7/26, 6:43:37 AM] Gary Teh: As suggested by Grok
  
  Awe (oneness with the universe) also has the effect of reducing aggression and increasing pro-social behavior
  [4/7/26, 6:48:04 AM] Gary Teh: Further suggestions from deep seek 
  
  Compassion is also a vibe to consider leaning into
  [4/7/26, 6:52:16 AM] Gary Teh: ChatGpt seems to concur with Gemini and goes on to point that Awe is a structural moat due to the nature of our mission
  [4/7/26, 6:57:53 AM] Gary Teh: Copilot seems to have also homed in on Awe as the structural moat of our position
  [4/7/26, 7:08:19 AM] Gary Teh: Claude went even further 
  
  Suggesting that compared to liquid death which utilizes humor our moat is Awe
  
  Utilization of anger, contempt and indignation while yielding short term spikes erodes trust and brand equity overtime 
  
  Also to give some thought as to the identity (counter culture) we are allowing supporters to subscribe to when they buy our cacao. 
  
  Our single estate product configuration makes us by default not a commodity
  
  And our nomadic network is a strategic asset which has helped us by pas…
  ‎Read more
  [4/7/26, 7:12:40 AM] Gary Teh: The emotional palette to utilize for our content strategy seems apparent after this round of experiment and assessment utilizing the LLMs
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
